title: Artifact
description: A typed output produced by a run. Artifacts carry provenance (which spec, which agent, which run) and a lifecycle status. Every promoted output in the system is an artifact of one of the listed types.

id.description: Stable identifier for the artifact. Format is up to the producing repo, but must be unique within that repo.

type.description: Artifact type. Determines which downstream consumers know how to interpret it.

spec_id.description: Identifier of the spec this artifact serves. Example: specs/0001-cognitive-delivery-control-plane/.

produced_by.description: Identifier of the agent or human that produced the artifact. Example: claude-opus-4-7 or a GitHub handle.

run_id.description: Identifier of the Run that produced this artifact. Lets consumers join back to the run record for cost, events, and inputs.

status.description: Lifecycle state. Proposed on creation; accepted on merge or review pass; superseded when a later artifact replaces it; rejected when the gate or reviewer turned it down.

summary.description: One- or two-sentence description of what the artifact is and why it exists.

evidence.description: Pointers to supporting artifacts, runs, or external references.
